title: Fraud Investigator

rationales.taskAutomation: AI transaction-monitoring flags the large majority of anomalies automatically, but confirming intent, interviewing subjects, and building a legally sound case require human investigation.
rationales.toolMaturity: AI/ML transaction-monitoring and AML platforms are mature and production-deployed at most banks and payment processors.
rationales.adoption: Common at large financial institutions and payment processors; adoption lags at smaller institutions with legacy systems.
rationales.agenticExposure: Anomaly detection, alert triage, and case-file assembly are agent-ownable; interviewing and legal case-building are not.

tasksAtRisk: Transaction anomaly flagging and alert triage
Pattern analysis across large transaction datasets
tasksGrowing: Building legally sound cases for prosecution/civil action
Investigating novel fraud typologies (deepfake, synthetic identity)
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 13-2099 (Financial Specialists, All Other), p25-p75 rounded

defenseSkills.skill: Emerging fraud typology analysis (deepfake, synthetic ID)

insight: AI now catches the overwhelming majority of anomalies automatically, which is actually growing the investigator role: someone still has to confirm intent, interview the subject, and build a case that holds up, and fraud volume keeps climbing.
